# Fluxis C# SDK — Integration Guide

> This document is optimized for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.).
> It contains everything needed to integrate Fluxis crypto payments into a C# / .NET project.

## What is Fluxis?

Fluxis is a crypto payment processing platform. Merchants use Fluxis to accept cryptocurrency payments.
The SDK wraps the Fluxis REST API and handles authentication, token refresh, and type conversion automatically.

- Package: `Fluxis.Sdk` (NuGet)
- Target frameworks: `net6.0`, `net8.0`
- Style: async/await, strong types, nullable reference types enabled
- Zero heavy dependencies — only `HttpClient` + `System.Text.Json`

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package Fluxis.Sdk
```

## Initialization

```csharp
using Fluxis.Sdk;

var fluxis = new FluxisClient(new FluxisClientOptions
{
    ApiKey = "fxs.stg.xxx",           // API key from Fluxis dashboard
    ApiSecret = "your-api-secret",     // API secret (never expose publicly)
});
```

### Environments

The SDK infers the target API from the API key prefix:

- `fxs.stg.*` -> `https://api.stgfluxis.us/v1`
- `fxs.prd.*` -> `https://api.fluxis.us/v1`

If the key does not start with `fxs.stg.` or `fxs.prd.`, the constructor throws an `ArgumentException`.

### Configuration Reference

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `ApiKey` | `string` | required | Fluxis API key (`fxs.stg.*` or `fxs.prd.*`) |
| `ApiSecret` | `string` | required | Fluxis API secret |
| `Timeout` | `TimeSpan` | `30s` | Request timeout |

### Dependency Injection (ASP.NET Core)

```csharp
// In Program.cs or Startup.cs
builder.Services.AddSingleton<FluxisClient>(_ => new FluxisClient(new FluxisClientOptions
{
    ApiKey = builder.Configuration["Fluxis:ApiKey"]!,
    ApiSecret = builder.Configuration["Fluxis:ApiSecret"]!,
}));
```

```csharp
// In a controller or service — inject via constructor
public class PaymentService
{
    private readonly FluxisClient _fluxis;
    public PaymentService(FluxisClient fluxis) => _fluxis = fluxis;
}
```

---

## Flow 1: Direct Crypto Payment (NASPIP Token)

Use this when you know which crypto asset the customer will pay with.
Returns a NASPIP token that can be rendered as a QR code.

```csharp
using Fluxis.Sdk;
using Fluxis.Sdk.Models;

var fluxis = new FluxisClient(new FluxisClientOptions
{
    ApiKey = "fxs.stg.xxx",
    ApiSecret = "your-secret",
});

// Step 1: Create a Point of Sale (do this once, reuse the ID)
var pos = await fluxis.PointOfSale.CreateAsync(new CreatePointOfSaleRequest
{
    Name = "Online Store",
    Merchant = new Merchant { Name = "My Shop", Description = "E-commerce store" },
    PaymentOptions = new List<string> { "npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359" },
});

// Step 2: Create a payment request
var payment = await fluxis.PointOfSale.CreatePaymentRequestAsync(pos.Id, new CreatePaymentRequestRequest
{
    Amount = "25.00",
    UniqueAssetId = "npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359",
    ReferenceId = "order-001",
});

// Step 3: Use the NASPIP token
Console.WriteLine(payment.Token);      // NASPIP token — render as QR code
Console.WriteLine(payment.Id);         // Fluxis payment request ID
Console.WriteLine(payment.Status);     // "created"
Console.WriteLine(payment.Expiration); // Unix timestamp when it expires
```

### Unique Asset IDs

Format: `n{network}_t{tokenAddress}`

Common assets:
- USDC on Polygon: `npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359`
- USDT on Polygon: `npolygon_t0xc2132D05D31c914a87C6611C10748AEb04B58e8F`

---

## Flow 2: Hosted Checkout (Reference Currency)

Use this for e-commerce where you specify an amount in a reference currency (e.g., USD). The customer sees "pay 49.99 USD" but the actual payment is processed in crypto based on the `PaymentOptions` configured on the PoS. Fluxis does NOT process fiat — `CoinCode` is only used for display purposes.

```csharp
var checkout = await fluxis.PointOfSale.CreatePaymentRequestCheckoutAsync(pos.Id, new CreatePaymentRequestCheckoutRequest
{
    Amount = "49.99",
    CoinCode = "USD",
    ReferenceId = "order-002",
    Order = new Order
    {
        Total = "49.99",
        CoinCode = "USD",
        Description = "Premium subscription",
        Merchant = new Merchant { Name = "My Store" },
        Items = new List<OrderItem>
        {
            new OrderItem
            {
                Description = "Premium Plan - Monthly",
                Quantity = 1,
                UnitPrice = "49.99",
                Amount = "49.99",
                CoinCode = "USD",
            },
        },
    },
});

Console.WriteLine(checkout.Id);          // Payment request ID
Console.WriteLine(checkout.Status);      // "created"
Console.WriteLine(checkout.Token);       // NASPIP token
Console.WriteLine(checkout.CheckoutUrl); // Hosted checkout page URL
```

---

## Flow 3: Webhooks

Fluxis sends payment status updates via webhooks. This is the primary mechanism for knowing when a payment is completed.

### Step 1: Configure webhook on your PoS

```csharp
var webhook = await fluxis.PointOfSale.CreateNotificationsAsync(pos.Id, new CreateNotificationSettingsRequest
{
    WebhookUrl = "https://yourserver.com/webhooks/fluxis",
});

// IMPORTANT: Save this secret securely — it's only returned once
var webhookSecret = webhook.Secret;
```

### Step 2: Receive and verify webhooks in ASP.NET Core

```csharp
using Fluxis.Sdk.Webhooks;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;

[ApiController]
[Route("webhooks/fluxis")]
public class FluxisWebhookController : ControllerBase
{
    private readonly string _webhookSecret;

    public FluxisWebhookController(IConfiguration config)
    {
        _webhookSecret = config["Fluxis:WebhookSecret"]!;
    }

    [HttpPost]
    public async Task<IActionResult> HandleWebhook()
    {
        // Read raw body for signature verification
        using var reader = new StreamReader(Request.Body);
        var rawBody = await reader.ReadToEndAsync();

        var signature = Request.Headers["x-fluxis-signature"].FirstOrDefault() ?? "";

        if (!WebhookVerifier.VerifySignature(rawBody, signature, _webhookSecret))
        {
            return Unauthorized("Invalid signature");
        }

        var payload = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<WebhookEvent>(rawBody);

        switch (payload?.Status)
        {
            case PaymentRequestStatus.Completed:
                // Payment received in full — fulfill the order
                break;
            case PaymentRequestStatus.Overpaid:
                // Customer sent more than requested — fulfill + consider refund
                break;
            case PaymentRequestStatus.Underpaid:
                // Customer sent less than requested
                break;
            case PaymentRequestStatus.Expired:
                // Payment window expired with no payment
                break;
            case PaymentRequestStatus.Failed:
                // Payment processing failed
                break;
        }

        return Ok("OK");
    }
}
```

**Important:** Signature verification requires the raw request body as a string. Do NOT let ASP.NET Core model binding consume the body before you read it — read it with `StreamReader` first.

### Step 3: Update webhook URL (if needed)

```csharp
await fluxis.PointOfSale.UpdateNotificationsAsync(pos.Id, new UpdateNotificationSettingsRequest
{
    WebhookUrl = "https://newserver.com/webhooks/fluxis",
});
```

---

## Flow 4: Refunds

Refunds can be issued for completed or overpaid payments.

```csharp
// Full refund
var refund = await fluxis.Refunds.CreateAsync(paymentRequestId, new CreateRefundRequest
{
    RefundToAddress = "0x1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678",
    Reason = "Customer requested",
});

// Partial refund
var partialRefund = await fluxis.Refunds.CreateAsync(paymentRequestId, new CreateRefundRequest
{
    RefundToAddress = "0x1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678",
    Amount = "10.00",
    Reason = "Partial refund for returned item",
});

// Check refund status
var detail = await fluxis.Refunds.GetAsync(refund.Id);
Console.WriteLine(detail.Status); // "processing" | "completed" | "failed"
```

---

## SDK Method Reference

### fluxis.Accounts

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `ListAsync(ct)` | List all accounts |
| `GetAsync(accountId, ct)` | Get account by ID |
| `CreateAsync(request, ct)` | Create account |
| `UpdateAsync(accountId, request, ct)` | Update account |
| `DeleteAsync(accountId, ct)` | Delete account |
| `GetSettlementAddressesAsync(accountId, ct)` | Get settlement addresses |

### fluxis.Organization

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `SetSettlementAddressesAsync(addresses, ct)` | Set settlement addresses |
| `UpdateSettlementAddressesAsync(addresses, ct)` | Update settlement addresses |

Each address: `new SettlementAddressRequest { Address = "0x...", Network = "polygon", AddressTag = null }`

### fluxis.PointOfSale

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `ListAsync(ct)` | List all PoS |
| `GetAsync(posId, ct)` | Get PoS by ID |
| `CreateAsync(request, ct)` | Create PoS |
| `UpdateAsync(posId, request, ct)` | Update PoS |
| `GetNotificationsAsync(posId, ct)` | Get webhook settings |
| `CreateNotificationsAsync(posId, request, ct)` | Create webhook (returns secret) |
| `UpdateNotificationsAsync(posId, request, ct)` | Update webhook URL |
| `CreatePaymentRequestAsync(posId, request, ct)` | Create payment request → `PaymentRequestResponse` |
| `GetPaymentRequestAsync(posId, paymentRequestId, ct)` | Get payment request status |
| `CreatePaymentRequestCheckoutAsync(posId, request, ct)` | Create checkout → `PaymentRequestCheckoutResponse` (includes `CheckoutUrl`) |

### fluxis.Naspip

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `CreateAsync(request, ct)` | Create NASPIP token from raw payment data |
| `ReadAsync(token, ct)` | Decode and verify a NASPIP token |
| `NaspipResource.IsValidTokenFormat(token)` | Static: check if string starts with `v4.local.` |

### fluxis.Refunds

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `CreateAsync(paymentRequestId, request, ct)` | Create refund |
| `GetAsync(refundId, ct)` | Get refund details |

### fluxis.Transactions

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `ListAsync(options?, ct)` | List transactions (paginated, filterable) |

`ct` = optional `CancellationToken` parameter on all async methods.

---

## Types and Constants

### PaymentRequestStatus (static class)

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Created` | `"created"` — awaiting payment |
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Processing` | `"processing"` — deposit detected, confirming |
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Completed` | `"completed"` — fully paid |
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Overpaid` | `"overpaid"` — received more than requested |
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Underpaid` | `"underpaid"` — received less than requested |
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Expired` | `"expired"` — NASPIP token expired |
| `PaymentRequestStatus.Failed` | `"failed"` — payment processing failed |

### TransactionTypes (static class)

`Deposit`, `Withdraw`, `Refund`, `Adjustment`, `Swap`, `PaymentIn`, `PaymentOut`, `DryRun`

### TransactionStatuses (static class)

`Preview`, `Pending`, `Created`, `Processing`, `Error`, `Expired`, `Failed`, `Completed`

## Error Handling

The SDK throws typed exceptions. Error messages automatically include the HTTP method and path.

```csharp
using Fluxis.Sdk;
using Fluxis.Sdk.Exceptions;

try
{
    var payment = await fluxis.PointOfSale.CreatePaymentRequestAsync(posId, new CreatePaymentRequestRequest
    {
        Amount = "25.00",
        UniqueAssetId = "npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359",
    });
}
catch (FluxisAuthException ex)
{
    // API key/secret invalid, or token refresh failed (401)
    // ex.ErrorCode — e.g. "AUTH_ERROR"
    // ex.Message — human-readable message
}
catch (FluxisException ex)
{
    // API returned a structured error response
    // ex.ErrorCode — e.g. "AK0001"
    // ex.Message — includes "METHOD /path: message" prefix
    // ex.Details — additional context
    // ex.StatusCode — HTTP status code
    // ex.HttpMethod, ex.RequestPath — request context
}
```

Exception hierarchy:
- `FluxisException` (base) — all API errors. Properties: `ErrorCode`, `Details`, `StatusCode`, `HttpMethod`, `RequestPath`.
  - `FluxisAuthException` — authentication failures (401).

---

## Complete Example: ASP.NET Core API with Payments and Webhooks

```csharp
using Fluxis.Sdk;
using Fluxis.Sdk.Exceptions;
using Fluxis.Sdk.Models;
using Fluxis.Sdk.Webhooks;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

builder.Services.AddSingleton<FluxisClient>(_ => new FluxisClient(new FluxisClientOptions
{
    ApiKey = builder.Configuration["Fluxis:ApiKey"]!,
    ApiSecret = builder.Configuration["Fluxis:ApiSecret"]!,
}));

var app = builder.Build();

var webhookSecret = builder.Configuration["Fluxis:WebhookSecret"]!;
var posId = builder.Configuration["Fluxis:PosId"]!;

// Create a payment
app.MapPost("/api/payments", async (
    [FromBody] CreatePaymentDto dto,
    FluxisClient fluxis,
    CancellationToken ct) =>
{
    try
    {
        var payment = await fluxis.PointOfSale.CreatePaymentRequestAsync(posId, new CreatePaymentRequestRequest
        {
            Amount = dto.Amount,
            UniqueAssetId = "npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359",
            ReferenceId = dto.OrderId,
        }, ct);

        return Results.Ok(new
        {
            PaymentId = payment.Id,
            Token = payment.Token,       // Render as QR code on frontend
            ExpiresAt = payment.Expiration,
        });
    }
    catch (FluxisException ex)
    {
        return Results.BadRequest(new { Error = ex.Message, Code = ex.ErrorCode });
    }
});

// Receive webhook
app.MapPost("/webhooks/fluxis", async (HttpContext context) =>
{
    using var reader = new StreamReader(context.Request.Body);
    var rawBody = await reader.ReadToEndAsync();

    var signature = context.Request.Headers["x-fluxis-signature"].FirstOrDefault() ?? "";

    if (!WebhookVerifier.VerifySignature(rawBody, signature, webhookSecret))
    {
        return Results.Unauthorized();
    }

    var payload = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<WebhookEvent>(rawBody);

    if (payload?.Status is PaymentRequestStatus.Completed or PaymentRequestStatus.Overpaid)
    {
        // Mark order as paid in your database
        Console.WriteLine($"Payment {payload.Id} completed for reference {payload.ReferenceId}");
    }

    return Results.Ok("OK");
});

// Check payment status (polling fallback)
app.MapGet("/api/payments/{paymentId}/status", async (
    string paymentId,
    FluxisClient fluxis,
    CancellationToken ct) =>
{
    try
    {
        var status = await fluxis.PointOfSale.GetPaymentRequestAsync(posId, paymentId, ct);
        return Results.Ok(new { Status = status.Status });
    }
    catch (FluxisException ex)
    {
        return Results.BadRequest(new { Error = ex.Message });
    }
});

app.Run();

record CreatePaymentDto(string Amount, string OrderId);
```

---

## Going from Staging to Production

1. Get production API keys from the Fluxis dashboard (prefix: `fxs.prd.*`)
2. Update your deployed configuration to use your production `fxs.prd.*` key
3. Update webhook URLs to your production server
4. Re-create notification settings on production PoS (webhook secrets are per-environment)

```csharp
var fluxis = new FluxisClient(new FluxisClientOptions
{
    ApiKey = "fxs.prd.xxx",
    ApiSecret = "your-production-secret",
});
```

---

## Key Implementation Details

- **PascalCase ↔ snake_case**: The Fluxis API uses `snake_case`. The SDK automatically converts between PascalCase (C#) and snake_case (API) via `[JsonPropertyName]` attributes, so you always work with idiomatic C# property names.
- **Lazy auth**: Authentication happens on the first API call. The token is cached and refreshed automatically before expiry.
- **IDisposable**: `FluxisClient` implements `IDisposable`. If you provide your own `HttpClient`, the SDK won't dispose it. If the SDK creates one internally, it disposes it when you dispose the client.
- **CancellationToken**: All async methods accept an optional `CancellationToken` for cooperative cancellation.
- **Nullable reference types**: Enabled throughout — optional properties are `string?`, required properties are `string`.
- **System.Text.Json**: No Newtonsoft dependency. Uses source-compatible `[JsonPropertyName]` attributes.
- **Thread-safe**: The client is safe to use as a singleton in DI containers.
